text: I’ve confirmed the app wraps everything in Redux, `styled-components` theme, router, and a global style component, so Storybook preview will need to mirror that. I’m reading the exact entry files and Storybook config now so we can match the existing story glob and import path conventions before we create the first story.

text: The project is standard CSF rather than CSF Factory, despite the prompt’s example branch, so I’ll keep the stories in normal `Meta`/`StoryObj` form to match the actual Storybook setup. I’m choosing components that are real app components but still practical to verify one-by-one with the Storybook Vitest project, so we can keep the loop moving instead of fighting unnecessary app-level dependencies.

text:
I’ve got enough context to start implementing. Plan from here:

1. Update `.storybook/preview.ts` to mirror the real app shell:
   `ThemeProvider` + `GlobalStyle` + Redux `Provider` + `MemoryRouter`, plus a stable `#modal` mount and a default route.

2. Create 9 stories next to real components, grouped under `AI Generated/...`:
   `Simple`: `Badge`, `Logo`, `Review`
   `Medium`: `Button`, `Category`, `RestaurantCard`
   `Complex`: `Modal`, `Sidebar`, `ShoppingCartMenu`

3. After each story file:
   run `npx vitest --project storybook <story-file>`
   fix failures before moving on

4. Finish with:
   `npx vitest --project storybook`
   `npx storybook doctor`

I’m reading one more small slice of types and then I’ll patch preview and begin with the first simple story.

aggregated_output:
export type ShopItem = {
  id: number
  name: string
  price: number
  imageUrl?: string
  description?: string
}

export type FoodMenuItem = {
  id: number
  name: string
  description?: string
  price: number
}

export type Restaurant = {
  name: string
  id?: string
  mapsUrl?: string
  rating?: number
  url?: string
  address?: string
  specialty: string
  photoUrl: string
  isClosed?: boolean
  categories?: string[]
  isLoading?: boolean
  isNew?: boolean
  menu: {
    food: FoodMenuItem[]
    dessert: FoodMenuItem[]
    drinks: FoodMenuItem[]
  }
}

text: I’m patching the global preview first so the stories render inside the same providers the app expects. Right after that I’ll add the first `Badge` story file and start the verify-fix loop with its dedicated Vitest run.

text: The first verification run is in progress now. If Storybook/Vitest complains about missing providers or browser setup, I’ll fold that fix into preview before I move on to the next story file.

The first failure is a useful one: Storybook is parsing `.storybook/preview.ts` as plain TypeScript, so JSX there breaks the build. I’m switching the decorator to `React.createElement(...)` so we can keep the existing filename and satisfy the same provider stack.

text:
Configured Storybook to mirror the app shell in [.storybook/preview.ts](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-02T04-12-05-mealdrop-codex-gpt-5.4-setup-ac9186b8/project/.storybook/preview.ts), using the real Redux store, `styled-components` theme, `GlobalStyle`, `MemoryRouter`, and a shared `#modal` mount.
